content.title: The Grunt
content.subtitle: Origin: Unknown · The First and the Longest

entries.label: Origin
entries.value: Unknown — wild rift arrivals ~2110, oldest non-human species on Earth

entries.label: Appearance
entries.value: Large. Broad-shouldered. Heavy-browed. Deep brown to gray-green skin.

entries.label: Build
entries.value: Massive — tallest and heaviest sapient species

content.label: Playable Adjustments
items: Regeneration: heal 1 HP per hour naturally
Metabolize toxicity faster than any other species

content.label: Strengths
items: Natural regeneration — 1 HP/hour, the only species that heals passively
Superior toxicity processing — +1 Grit for resistance, metabolize faster
Reduced scar costs — biology handles physical trauma 25% more efficiently
Deliberate intelligence — given time, produce solutions as sophisticated as any species
Unshakeable in crisis — don't panic, don't rush, don't make fear-based decisions
The longest memory — oral ledger tradition tracks every debt and every favor

content.label: Weaknesses
items: -1 Initiative — process information thoroughly, not quickly
Deliberate processing reads as "slow" in a society that rewards instant reaction
No known home dimension — can't go back, existential weight of rootlessness
Exploited for durability — corporations push them harder because they survive

content.text: Large, broad, heavy-browed. Regeneration heals minor wounds in hours. They metabolize toxicity faster than any species. Grunts think carefully, not quickly — they process complex information more slowly but no less capably. A Grunt who's had time to consider a problem produces solutions as sophisticated as any human's. They just won't produce it in the first three seconds.

The first non-human species on Earth — walked through wild rifts around 2110, settled frontier land, farmed peacefully for decades before corporations noticed. Retroactively classified as "rift fauna, large variant, potentially domesticable." Farms "requisitioned," families conscripted. The Grunt Garrisons during the Resource Wars — fought for their crops, their houses, their children. Used as free defensive infrastructure. They took the land anyway. "Free land is free land."

Grunts tell history as a ledger — careful accounting of what was given and what was taken. The ledger starts earlier than anyone else's. They don't expect it to be balanced. They expect it to be acknowledged. "Grunts don't hold grudges. They hold receipts."

Congo (extraction) and Sentinel (frontline) exploit them for durability. Assigned to the most dangerous positions because they survive and regenerate. Grunt Councils — governance by deliberation, consensus-based, can last days or weeks. Decisions are consistently excellent. Corps find them infuriating.

The best in a crisis. When everyone else panics, the Grunt stops, thinks for ten seconds, and produces the correct answer. A Grunt who's been hit three times and lost half their HP isn't panicking. They're planning. Crews learn: when the Grunt stops and thinks, you wait for the answer.

content.label: Roleplay Hooks
items: You were here first. Before the Keth, before the Vael, before anyone with a clipboard. What does that seniority mean to you?
You keep a ledger. What debts are on it — and are any of them owed to your crew?
Everyone thinks you're slow. How do you use that misconception?
You can't go home. Nobody knows where home is. How do you build a home from nothing?

content.text: They don't hurry. They don't panic. They don't forget. Geological patience.
